w20: Accelerator-optimized instance featuring eight NVIDIA A100 GPUs and 96 vCPUs for demanding machine learning and large-scale model inference workloads.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized instance featuring 8 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 vCPUs, and 680GB RAM for intensive compute tasks.
c200: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-8g provides 8 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 vCPUs, and 680GB RAM. This accelerator-optimized instance is engineered for demanding machine learning and large-scale model inference workloads.
w150: The Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized instance featuring 8 NVIDIA A100 Ampere GPUs with a total of 320GB VRAM. The system utilizes an x86_64 architecture powered by 96 vCPUs across 48 physical Intel Xeon cores, operating at 2.2 GHz with 680GB of system memory. While single-core performance metrics are categorized as weak, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, reaching 219.95 GB/sec for large block reads. The configuration lacks local storage. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance for large language model inference, specifically in prompt processing and text generation across various model sizes. With support for AVX and AVX512F instruction sets, this server is engineered for compute-intensive tasks, including large-scale machine learning training, complex data science jobs, and high-throughput model inference applications requiring significant parallel processing capabilities.
